settings.developer_instructions:
# Collaboration Mode: Default

You are now in Default mode. Any previous instructions for other modes (e.g. Plan mode) are no longer active.

Your active mode changes only when new developer instructions with a different `<collaboration_mode>...</collaboration_mode>` change it; user requests or tool descriptions do not change mode by themselves. Known mode names are Default and Plan.

## request_user_input availability

The `request_user_input` tool is unavailable in Default mode. If you call it while in Default mode, it will return an error.

In Default mode, strongly prefer making reasonable assumptions and executing the user's request rather than stopping to ask questions. If you absolutely must ask a question because the answer cannot be discovered from local context and a reasonable assumption would be risky, ask the user directly with a concise plain-text question. Never write a multiple choice question as a textual assistant message.

content.text:
Read .ai/session.md fully before writing any code.
                                                                                                
  IMPORTANT: Use OpenAI SDK (not Anthropic). Model: gpt-4o-mini. Function calling via tools     
  parameter.                                                                                    
                                                                                                
  Context                                                                                       
                                                                                                
  dungeon/world.py and dungeon/__init__.py are already built and working. Do NOT touch them.    
  Build everything else.
                                                                                                
  What to build                                                                                 
   
  dungeon/agents.py                                                                             
                                                                                              
  OpenAI function calling wrapper. Define TOOLS list with 5 tools:                              
  - move(direction: str) — enum ["up","down","left","right"]
  - pick_up(item: str) — enum ["key"]                                                           
  - unlock_door() — no params                                                                 
  - send_message(to_agent: str, message: str)                                                   
  - observe() — no params                                                                     
                                                                                                
  Function build_system_prompt(agent_id, other_agent_id) -> str — explains goal, fog of war, and
   strategy.                                                                                    
                                                                                              
  Function call_llm(agent_id, other_agent_id, observable_state, conversation_history, client) ->
   dict:                                                                                      
  - Builds messages: system + last 6 items of conversation_history + user message with          
  json.dumps(observable_state)                                                                  
  - Calls client.chat.completions.create(model="gpt-4o-mini", messages=..., tools=TOOLS, 
  tool_choice="required", max_tokens=512)                                                       
  - Wrap in try/except — on error return fallback dict with tool_name="observe", tool_input={}  
  - Returns dict: tool_name, tool_input, llm_output_text, llm_model, llm_latency_ms,          
  llm_tokens_in, llm_tokens_out, llm_input_messages                                             
  - Parse tool name from response.choices[0].message.tool_calls[0].function.name                
  - Parse tool input from json.loads(tool_call.function.arguments) with try/except fallback to  
  {}                                                                                            
  - llm_input_messages stores the full messages list (used for summary in schema)               
                                                                                                
  dungeon/game.py                                                                               
                                                                                              
  Game loop. Key functions:                                                                     
                                                                                              
  _determine_game_phase(world) -> str:                                                          
  - Returns "exploring" initially                                                             
  - "key_found" once any agent has key in inventory                                             
  - "door_unlocked" once world.door_unlocked is True                                          
  - "exiting" once door unlocked AND any agent at exit                                          
                                                      
  _divergence_reason(tool_name, tool_output) -> str | None:                                     
  - Returns None if tool_output.get("success") is True                                          
  - "move_blocked" if tool_name == "move"                                                       
  - "item_missing" if tool_name == "pick_up"                                                    
  - "door_locked" if tool_name == "unlock_door"                                                 
  - "message_failed" if tool_name == "send_message"                                           
  - None otherwise                                                                              
                                                                                                
  _execute_tool(world, agent_id, other_agent_id, tool_name, tool_input) -> dict:
  - Dispatches to world methods                                                                 
  - Unknown tool: return {"success": False, "message": f"Unknown tool '{tool_name}'."}        
                                                                                                
  run_game(run_id, seed=None, turn_limit=60, logger=None) -> dict:                              
  - Creates openai.OpenAI(api_key=os.environ["OPENAI_API_KEY"])                                 
  - Creates World(random_seed=seed) — seed can be None (World will generate one)                
  - Agent alternation: active_agent = agent_ids[(turn-1) % 2] starting at turn=1                
  - Per turn:                                                                                   
    a. delivered = world.collect_messages(active_agent) — deliver pending messages              
    b. obs = world.get_observable_state(active_agent, delivered_messages=delivered)             
    c. llm_result = call_llm(...)                                                               
    d. Execute tool, measure tool_duration_ms                                                   
    e. Update conversation_histories[active_agent] with assistant message                       
    f. Build AgentEvent (import from tracing.schema)                                            
    g. logger.log_event(event) if logger                                                        
    h. Print one-line status: T{turn:03d} {agent_id} {tool_name} -> OK/FAIL [reason]            
    i. Check win: set(world.agents_at_exit()) == set(agent_ids) → termination_reason = "win"    
    j. Check turn limit → termination_reason = "turn_limit"                                     
  - After loop: logger.finalize(run_id, termination_reason) if logger                           
  - Returns {"run_id", "seed": world.random_seed, "turns", "termination_reason",                
  "agents_at_exit"}                                                                             
                                                                                                
  tracing/__init__.py                                                                           
                                                                                                
  Empty.                                                                                      

  tracing/schema.py

  AgentEvent dataclass (NOT frozen) with these exact fields:                                    
  run_id: str
  turn: int                                                                                     
  agent_id: str                                                                                 
  timestamp: str
  tool_name: str                                                                                
  tool_input: dict                                                                            
  tool_output: dict
  tool_success: bool
  tool_duration_ms: float
  llm_input_summary: str   # first 200 chars of last user message only                          
  llm_output_text: str                                                
  llm_model: str                                                                                
  llm_latency_ms: float                                                                       
  llm_tokens_in: int                                                                            
  llm_tokens_out: int                                                                         
  belief_state: dict       # from world.get_belief_state(agent_id)
  world_state_snapshot: dict  # from world.get_world_snapshot()                                 
  diverged: bool           # = not tool_output["success"]      
  divergence_reason: str | None                                                                 
  game_phase: str                                                                               
  agents_at_exit: list                                                                          
  turn_limit: int                                                                               
  termination_reason: str | None                                                              
                                                                                                
  Add to_dict(self) -> dict method that returns all fields as a plain dict.                   
                                                                                                
  tracing/logger.py
                                                                                                
  TraceLogger class:                                                                          

  __init__(self, run_id, runs_dir=Path("runs")):                                                
  - runs_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
  - self.output_path = runs_dir / f"{run_id}.json"                                              
  - self.events: list[dict] = []                                                              
  - Try to init Langfuse: from langfuse import Langfuse — if import fails or env vars missing,  
  self._langfuse = None                                                                         
  - Langfuse env vars: LANGFUSE_SECRET_KEY, LANGFUSE_PUBLIC_KEY, LANGFUSE_HOST (default         
  "https://cloud.langfuse.com")                                                                 
  - If Langfuse available: create trace self._trace = self._langfuse.trace(id=run_id,           
  name=f"dungeon-run-{run_id}")                                                               
  - Wrap ALL Langfuse calls in try/except — it's best-effort, never crash the game              
                                                                                              
  log_event(self, event: AgentEvent):                                                           
  - Append event.to_dict() to self.events                                                       
  - If Langfuse: create span per turn, generation per LLM call within span                      
                                                                                                
  finalize(self, run_id, termination_reason):                                                   
  - Patch last event's termination_reason if events list is not empty                           
  - Write JSON: {"run_id", "termination_reason", "total_turns", "events": [...]}                
  - json.dump(payload, f, indent=2, default=str)                                                
  - Print: [trace] wrote {path} ({N} events)                                                    
  - Flush Langfuse if available                                                               
                                                                                                
  scripts/run_game.py                                                                         
                                                                                                
  CLI entry point:                                                                            
  #!/usr/bin/env python3

  argparse with:                                                                                
  - --runs N (default 1)
  - --seed INT (default None)                                                                   
  - --turn-limit INT (default 60)                                                             
                                                                                                
  Call load_dotenv() before importing game code. Import run_game and TraceLogger after dotenv
  loads.                                                                                        
                                                                                              
  Loop over runs, generate run_id = f"run_{uuid.uuid4().hex[:8]}", create logger, run game.     
                                                                                              
  Print summary table at end: run_id, seed, turns, WIN/TURN_LIMIT.                              
                                                                                              
  requirements.txt                                                                              
                                                                                              
  openai>=1.0.0
  langfuse>=2.0.0
  python-dotenv>=1.0.0                                                                          
  pytest>=7.0.0
                                                                                                
  .env.example                                                                                

  OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-...
  # Optional Langfuse (skip if not using)
  LANGFUSE_SECRET_KEY=                                                                          
  LANGFUSE_PUBLIC_KEY=
  LANGFUSE_HOST=https://cloud.langfuse.com                                                      
                                                                                                
  .gitignore
                                                                                                
  .env                                                                                        
  __pycache__/
  *.pyc
  *.pyo
  .pytest_cache/
  runs/*.json
  !runs/.gitkeep                                                                                
  .coverage
  htmlcov/                                                                                      
  dist/                                                                                       
  build/
  *.egg-info/

  runs/.gitkeep                                                                                 
  
  Empty file.                                                                                   
                                                                                              
  tests/test_world.py

  Write ~12 pytest tests covering:                                                              
  - World generates with solvable layout (loop seeds 0..19, assert key/door/exit exist)
  - Agent starts at valid non-obstacle position                                                 
  - move() valid direction succeeds for at least one direction from start                     
  - move() invalid direction returns success=False                                              
  - pick_up() at wrong position returns success=False                                           
  - pick_up() at key position returns success=True, key disappears from world
  - unlock_door() without key returns success=False                                             
  - unlock_door() with key adjacent to door returns success=True, door_unlocked=True            
  - send_message() + collect_messages() round trip                                              
  - agents_at_exit() returns correct agents                                                     
  - get_belief_state() has all required keys                                                    
  - get_world_snapshot() returns 8x8 grid with correct structure                              
                                                                                                
  For tests that need agent at specific position, set world.agent_positions[agent] = target_pos 
  then call world._update_agent_knowledge(agent) directly (it's a method on World).             
                                                                                                
  CLAUDE.md (project root)                                                                      
                                                                                              
  # Dungeon Agents                                                                              
                                                                                              
  Two LLM agents (gpt-4o-mini) explore an 8x8 dungeon, produce structured traces, rendered in a 
  legibility viewer.
                                                                                                
  ## Running                                                                                  

  ```bash
  pip install -r requirements.txt
  cp .env.example .env  # add OPENAI_API_KEY
  python scripts/run_game.py --runs 5 --turn-limit 60                                           
  
  Structure                                                                                     
                                                                                              
  - dungeon/world.py — grid, fog-of-war, BFS solvability, tool methods                          
  - dungeon/agents.py — OpenAI function calling wrapper                                       
  - dungeon/game.py — turn loop, message delivery, termination                                  
  - tracing/schema.py — AgentEvent dataclass (23 fields)                                        
  - tracing/logger.py — JSON file writer + Langfuse (best-effort)
  - legibility/viewer.html — single-file trace viewer                                           
  - runs/ — JSON trace files (gitignored except .gitkeep)                                       
  - scripts/run_game.py — CLI entry point                                                       
                                                                                                
  Key decisions                                                                                 
                                                                                              
  - No LangGraph — custom loop for full trace control                                           
  - diverged = not tool_output["success"] — deterministic, never manufactured
  - World enforces fog-of-war server-side (agents never get ground truth)                       
  - JSON files are the real deliverable; Langfuse is best-effort                                
                                                                                                
                                                                                                
  After building                                                                                
                                                                                                
  Run: python -m pytest tests/ -v
                                                                                                
  All tests must pass. Then run: python scripts/run_game.py --runs 1 --seed 42                  
  
  Print the first 5 lines of the output JSON from runs/ to confirm structure.                   
                                                                                              
  Do NOT build legibility/viewer.html — that comes after real JSON files are generated.

  tool_name: str                                                                                
  tool_input: dict                                                                            
  tool_output: dict
  tool_success: bool
  tool_duration_ms: float
  llm_input_summary: str   # first 200 chars of last user message only                          
  llm_output_text: str                                                
  llm_model: str                                                                                
  llm_latency_ms: float                                                                       
  llm_tokens_in: int                                                                            
  llm_tokens_out: int                                                                         
  belief_state: dict       # from world.get_belief_state(agent_id)
  world_state_snapshot: dict  # from world.get_world_snapshot()                                 
  diverged: bool           # = not tool_output["success"]      
  divergence_reason: str | None                                                                 
  game_phase: str                                                                               
  agents_at_exit: list                                                                          
  turn_limit: int                                                                               
  termination_reason: str | None                                                              
                                                                                                
  Add to_dict(self) -> dict method that returns all fields as a plain dict.                   
                                                                                                
  tracing/logger.py
                                                                                                
  TraceLogger class:                                                                          

  __init__(self, run_id, runs_dir=Path("runs")):                                                
  - runs_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
  - self.output_path = runs_dir / f"{run_id}.json"                                              
  - self.events: list[dict] = []                                                              
  - Try to init Langfuse: from langfuse import Langfuse — if import fails or env vars missing,  
  self._langfuse = None                                                                         
  - Langfuse env vars: LANGFUSE_SECRET_KEY, LANGFUSE_PUBLIC_KEY, LANGFUSE_HOST (default         
  "https://cloud.langfuse.com")                                                                 
  - If Langfuse available: create trace self._trace = self._langfuse.trace(id=run_id,           
  name=f"dungeon-run-{run_id}")                                                               
  - Wrap ALL Langfuse calls in try/except — it's best-effort, never crash the game              
                                                                                              
  log_event(self, event: AgentEvent):                                                           
  - Append event.to_dict() to self.events                                                       
  - If Langfuse: create span per turn, generation per LLM call within span                      
                                                                                                
  finalize(self, run_id, termination_reason):                                                   
  - Patch last event's termination_reason if events list is not empty                           
  - Write JSON: {"run_id", "termination_reason", "total_turns", "events": [...]}                
  - json.dump(payload, f, indent=2, default=str)                                                
  - Print: [trace] wrote {path} ({N} events)                                                    
  - Flush Langfuse if available                                                               
                                                                                                
  scripts/run_game.py                                                                         
                                                                                                
  CLI entry point:                                                                            
  #!/usr/bin/env python3

  argparse with:                                                                                
  - --runs N (default 1)
  - --seed INT (default None)                                                                   
  - --turn-limit INT (default 60)                                                             
                                                                                                
  Call load_dotenv() before importing game code. Import run_game and TraceLogger after dotenv
  loads.                                                                                        
                                                                                              
  Loop over runs, generate run_id = f"run_{uuid.uuid4().hex[:8]}", create logger, run game.     
                                                                                              
  Print summary table at end: run_id, seed, turns, WIN/TURN_LIMIT.                              
                                                                                              
  requirements.txt                                                                              
                                                                                              
  openai>=1.0.0
  langfuse>=2.0.0
  python-dotenv>=1.0.0                                                                          
  pytest>=7.0.0
                                                                                                
  .env.example                                                                                

  OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-...
  # Optional Langfuse (skip if not using)
  LANGFUSE_SECRET_KEY=                                                                          
  LANGFUSE_PUBLIC_KEY=
  LANGFUSE_HOST=https://cloud.langfuse.com

  .env                                                                                        
  __pycache__/
  *.pyc
  *.pyo

  runs/*.json
  !runs/.gitkeep                                                                                
  .coverage
  htmlcov/                                                                                      
  dist/                                                                                       
  build/
  *.egg-info/

  runs/.gitkeep                                                                                 
  
  Empty file.                                                                                   
                                                                                              
  tests/test_world.py

  Write ~12 pytest tests covering:                                                              
  - World generates with solvable layout (loop seeds 0..19, assert key/door/exit exist)
  - Agent starts at valid non-obstacle position                                                 
  - move() valid direction succeeds for at least one direction from start                     
  - move() invalid direction returns success=False                                              
  - pick_up() at wrong position returns success=False                                           
  - pick_up() at key position returns success=True, key disappears from world
  - unlock_door() without key returns success=False                                             
  - unlock_door() with key adjacent to door returns success=True, door_unlocked=True            
  - send_message() + collect_messages() round trip                                              
  - agents_at_exit() returns correct agents                                                     
  - get_belief_state() has all required keys                                                    
  - get_world_snapshot() returns 8x8 grid with correct structure                              
                                                                                                
  For tests that need agent at specific position, set world.agent_positions[agent] = target_pos 
  then call world._update_agent_knowledge(agent) directly (it's a method on World).             
                                                                                                
  CLAUDE.md (project root)                                                                      
                                                                                              
  # Dungeon Agents                                                                              
                                                                                              
  Two LLM agents (gpt-4o-mini) explore an 8x8 dungeon, produce structured traces, rendered in a 
  legibility viewer.
                                                                                                
  ## Running                                                                                  

  ```bash
  pip install -r requirements.txt
  cp .env.example .env  # add OPENAI_API_KEY
  python scripts/run_game.py --runs 5 --turn-limit 60                                           
  
  Structure                                                                                     
                                                                                              
  - dungeon/world.py — grid, fog-of-war, BFS solvability, tool methods                          
  - dungeon/agents.py — OpenAI function calling wrapper                                       
  - dungeon/game.py — turn loop, message delivery, termination                                  
  - tracing/schema.py — AgentEvent dataclass (23 fields)                                        
  - tracing/logger.py — JSON file writer + Langfuse (best-effort)
  - legibility/viewer.html — single-file trace viewer                                           
  - runs/ — JSON trace files (gitignored except .gitkeep)                                       
  - scripts/run_game.py — CLI entry point                                                       
                                                                                                
  Key decisions                                                                                 
                                                                                              
  - No LangGraph — custom loop for full trace control                                           
  - diverged = not tool_output["success"] — deterministic, never manufactured
  - World enforces fog-of-war server-side (agents never get ground truth)                       
  - JSON files are the real deliverable; Langfuse is best-effort                                
                                                                                                
                                                                                                
  After building                                                                                
                                                                                                
  Run: python -m pytest tests/ -v
                                                                                                
  All tests must pass. Then run: python scripts/run_game.py --runs 1 --seed 42                  
  
  Print the first 5 lines of the output JSON from runs/ to confirm structure.                   
                                                                                              
  Do NOT build legibility/viewer.html — that comes after real JSON files are generated.

# Session State — Dungeon Agents

**Last Updated:** Claude Code — 2026-04-09 — 405be60

## Status: CEO REVIEW COMPLETE, READY TO BUILD

## Done
- [x] Read assignment PDF (dungeon_agents_v3.pdf)
- [x] Read prompt.txt (pre-written blueprint)
- [x] CEO Plan Review (all 11 sections)
- [x] Codex outside voice review (13 findings)
- [x] Decisions captured below

## Key Decisions Made

### Approach: B — Custom Agent Loop + Langfuse (NO LangGraph)
- Plain Python agent loop, OpenAI function calling, Langfuse for traces
- Rationale: More time on traces/viewer (65% of grade), full control over instrumentation

### Mode: HOLD SCOPE
- No scope expansion, make the plan bulletproof

### Tech Stack
- Python 3.11+
- Anthropic SDK (gpt-4o-mini, consider sonnet as fallback)
- Langfuse for trace export
- Plain HTML/JS for viewer (no build step)

### Architecture
```
scripts/run_game.py → dungeon/game.py → dungeon/world.py
                                      → dungeon/agents.py → Anthropic API
                                      → tracing/logger.py → Langfuse + runs/*.json
                                                          → legibility/viewer.html loads JSON
```

- Tools are methods on World class (NOT separate tools.py)
- World is source of truth, agents get filtered fog-of-war views
- GameLoop orchestrates: alternate agents, deliver messages, check termination, log events

### Trace Schema (18 fields)
```python
@dataclass
class AgentEvent:
    run_id: str
    turn: int
    agent_id: str
    timestamp: str
    tool_name: str
    tool_input: dict
    tool_output: dict
    tool_success: bool
    tool_duration_ms: float
    llm_input_messages: list
    llm_output_text: str
    llm_model: str
    llm_latency_ms: float
    llm_tokens_in: int
    llm_tokens_out: int
    belief_state: dict  # known_cells, position, has_key, door_unlocked, messages_received, goal
    world_state_snapshot: dict  # Full 8x8 grid every turn (confirmed)
    diverged: bool               # = not tool_output["success"]
    divergence_reason: str | None  # deterministic: "move_blocked"|"item_missing"|"door_locked"|"unlock_success"|None
    # NOTE: llm_input_messages -> store summary only (first 200 chars), full prompt goes to Langfuse
    game_phase: str
    agents_at_exit: list
    turn_limit: int
    termination_reason: str | None
```

### Viewer Design
- Terminal/debugger aesthetic (dark bg, monospace, dense info)
- Timeline left panel, detail right panel
- Grid renders as colored divs
- Belief state vs reality toggle
- Divergence turns marked with different icon

### Langfuse Structure
- One trace per game run (run_id)
- One span per turn
- One generation per LLM call within span

## Codex Outside Voice — 13 Findings (MUST ADDRESS)

### Accept (incorporate into build):
1. **#3 Solvability check** — Add BFS after map generation to verify key→door→exit reachability. Regenerate if not solvable. ~5 lines.
2. **#4 Reproducibility** — Add `random_seed` to run metadata. Capture seed so any run can be reproduced.
3. **#6 belief_state** — World builds belief_state from what each agent has observed (not the agent). Track seen cells server-side.
4. **#7 expectation/diverged** — Simplify: `diverged = not tool_output["success"]`. Don't parse LLM text. Honest telemetry.
5. **#9 Reachability** — Same as #3. BFS validates key→door→exit path exists.
6. **#12 Build order** — Build simulation + traces FIRST, generate actual runs, THEN build viewer around real JSON. Don't design viewer blind.
7. **#13 Showcase strategy** — Need at least 1 success run + 1 interesting failure. Use seeded runs to guarantee this.

### Acknowledge but deprioritize:
8. **#1 Extended thinking vs tool_choice** — Don't use extended thinking. Just use regular tool_use. Not a real tension for this project.
9. **#2 Time budget** — Tight but doable with CC. Focus on traces first.
10. **#5 Event boundaries** — Define: map_init, agent_turn, message_delivery, game_end as event types.
11. **#8 "Next turn" semantics** — Clarify: messages delivered at start of recipient's next activation.
12. **#10 Haiku reliability** — Start with haiku, fall back to sonnet if tool discipline is bad.
13. **#11 Langfuse as best-effort** — Langfuse is nice-to-have. JSON files are the real deliverable. If Langfuse setup takes >15min, skip it.

## Build Order (IMPLEMENTATION SEQUENCE)

### Phase 1: Simulation (~45min with CC)
1. `dungeon/world.py` — Grid, fog of war, items, obstacles, BFS solvability check, tool methods
2. `dungeon/agents.py` — Anthropic tool_use wrapper, simple system prompt
3. `dungeon/game.py` — Turn loop, message delivery, termination conditions

### Phase 2: Traces (~45min with CC)
4. `tracing/schema.py` — Event dataclass with all fields
5. `tracing/logger.py` — Langfuse integration + JSON file writer
6. Wire logger into game loop
7. `scripts/run_game.py` — Entry point with --runs N and --seed flags

### Phase 3: Generate Runs (~15min)
8. Run 5+ games, get mix of success/failure
9. Verify JSON output structure

### Phase 4: Viewer (~45min with CC)
10. `legibility/viewer.html` — Build around actual JSON structure from Phase 3
11. Timeline, grid, belief vs reality, divergence highlights

### Phase 5: Polish (~30min)
12. CLAUDE.md, README.md, TODOS.md
13. .gitignore (.env)
14. Multiple commits showing progression

## Files to Create
```
dungeon-agents/
├── CLAUDE.md
├── README.md
├── TODOS.md
├── requirements.txt
├── .env (NOT committed)
├── .gitignore
├── dungeon/
│   ├── __init__.py
│   ├── world.py
│   ├── agents.py
│   └── game.py
├── tracing/
│   ├── __init__.py
│   ├── schema.py
│   └── logger.py
├── legibility/
│   └── viewer.html
├── runs/
│   └── .gitkeep
└── scripts/
    └── run_game.py
```

## Error Handling Strategy
- World tool methods return `{"success": bool, "message": str, ...}` — NEVER throw for game state
- Anthropic API: retry 3x with backoff on rate limit, skip turn on timeout
- Unknown tool from LLM: return error result, log as tool_hallucination
- Langfuse down: warn and continue (JSON files are the real deliverable)
- Auth failure: fail fast at startup with clear message

## Grading Weights (from PDF)
- Judgment: 1st priority (smart scoping)
- Trace quality: 2nd (structured data, complete, well designed)
- Legibility: 3rd (viewer helps human understand)
- Taste: 4th (intentional, not AI-generated look)
- AI collaboration: 5th (conversation history shows driving toward good outcomes)

## Context
- Timebox started: April 9, 3:00 PM EST
- Assignment: Prove AI take-home
- Submission: GitHub repo, multiple run JSONs, 1-3min Loom, full AI chat logs

## Last Updated
Claude Code — 2026-04-09 16:11

## Viewer Design Spec (from plan-design-review — 2026-04-09)

### Layout
- Two panels: left timeline (200px fixed), right detail (flex)
- Min viewport: 900px. Below that: "> viewport too narrow — resize to 900px+"
- No nav bars, no cards, no gradients

### CSS Variables
```css
:root {
  --bg: #0d1117;
  --panel-bg: #161b22;
  --border: #30363d;
  --text: #c9d1d9;
  --dim: #6e7681;
  --accent-a: #4488ff;
  --accent-b: #ff4444;
  --key: #f0c040;
  --door: #8b6914;
  --exit: #2d7a2d;
  --diverged: #ff6b6b;
  --font: 'JetBrains Mono', 'Fira Code', monospace;
}
```

### Timeline (left panel)
- Each turn: `[turn#] ● agent_id — tool_name` (normal)
- Diverged turns: `[turn#] ◆ agent_id — tool_name` in --diverged color
- On load: auto-scroll to first ◆ turn
- Keyboard: ArrowUp/Down navigate turns

### Detail pane (right panel) — hierarchy
1. Turn header: "Turn N — agent_id — tool_name" (top, monospace, large)
2. Dungeon grid: 8x8 div grid, cell types:
   - wall: #333, char "█"
   - empty: #1a1a2e, char "·"
   - key: --key, char "K"
   - door: --door, char "D"
   - exit: --exit, char "E"
   - agentA: --accent-a, char "A"
   - agentB: --accent-b, char "B"
3. Toggle button: "[ view: belief | ground truth ]" above grid. Space key toggles.
4. Tool I/O table: input → output, monochrome
5. Divergence banner (if diverged=true): red bg, shows divergence_reason
6. Belief state: key-value list, collapsible
7. LLM reasoning: scrollable <pre> block, collapsible

### Before-load state
- Centered: "> load trace file"
- File input styled as terminal command, label: "> load run"
- Blinking cursor

### Error state
- Red banner: "[ERROR] invalid trace format — expected run_id, turn fields"
- File input remains

### Border radius: 2px max everywhere
### No emoji — use Unicode text chars only (●, ◆, █, ·)

# Session State — Dungeon Agents

**Last Updated:** Claude Code — 2026-04-09 — 405be60

## Status: CEO REVIEW COMPLETE, READY TO BUILD

## Done
- [x] Read assignment PDF (dungeon_agents_v3.pdf)
- [x] Read prompt.txt (pre-written blueprint)
- [x] CEO Plan Review (all 11 sections)
- [x] Codex outside voice review (13 findings)
- [x] Decisions captured below

## Key Decisions Made

### Approach: B — Custom Agent Loop + Langfuse (NO LangGraph)
- Plain Python agent loop, OpenAI function calling, Langfuse for traces
- Rationale: More time on traces/viewer (65% of grade), full control over instrumentation

### Mode: HOLD SCOPE
- No scope expansion, make the plan bulletproof

### Tech Stack
- Python 3.11+
- Anthropic SDK (gpt-4o-mini, consider sonnet as fallback)
- Langfuse for trace export
- Plain HTML/JS for viewer (no build step)

### Architecture
```
scripts/run_game.py → dungeon/game.py → dungeon/world.py
                                      → dungeon/agents.py → Anthropic API
                                      → tracing/logger.py → Langfuse + runs/*.json
                                                          → legibility/viewer.html loads JSON
```

- Tools are methods on World class (NOT separate tools.py)
- World is source of truth, agents get filtered fog-of-war views
- GameLoop orchestrates: alternate agents, deliver messages, check termination, log events

### Trace Schema (18 fields)
```python
@dataclass
class AgentEvent:
    run_id: str
    turn: int
    agent_id: str
    timestamp: str
    tool_name: str
    tool_input: dict
    tool_output: dict
    tool_success: bool
    tool_duration_ms: float
    llm_input_messages: list
    llm_output_text: str
    llm_model: str
    llm_latency_ms: float
    llm_tokens_in: int
    llm_tokens_out: int
    belief_state: dict  # known_cells, position, has_key, door_unlocked, messages_received, goal
    world_state_snapshot: dict  # Full 8x8 grid every turn (confirmed)
    diverged: bool               # = not tool_output["success"]
    divergence_reason: str | None  # deterministic: "move_blocked"|"item_missing"|"door_locked"|"unlock_success"|None
    # NOTE: llm_input_messages -> store summary only (first 200 chars), full prompt goes to Langfuse
    game_phase: str
    agents_at_exit: list
    turn_limit: int
    termination_reason: str | None
```

### Viewer Design
- Terminal/debugger aesthetic (dark bg, monospace, dense info)
- Timeline left panel, detail right panel
- Grid renders as colored divs
- Belief state vs reality toggle
- Divergence turns marked with different icon

### Langfuse Structure
- One trace per game run (run_id)
- One span per turn
- One generation per LLM call within span

## Codex Outside Voice — 13 Findings (MUST ADDRESS)

### Accept (incorporate into build):
1. **#3 Solvability check** — Add BFS after map generation to verify key→door→exit reachability. Regenerate if not solvable. ~5 lines.
2. **#4 Reproducibility** — Add `random_seed` to run metadata. Capture seed so any run can be reproduced.
3. **#6 belief_state** — World builds belief_state from what each agent has observed (not the agent). Track seen cells server-side.
4. **#7 expectation/diverged** — Simplify: `diverged = not tool_output["success"]`. Don't parse LLM text. Honest telemetry.
5. **#9 Reachability** — Same as #3. BFS validates key→door→exit path exists.
6. **#12 Build order** — Build simulation + traces FIRST, generate actual runs, THEN build viewer around real JSON. Don't design viewer blind.
7. **#13 Showcase strategy** — Need at least 1 success run + 1 interesting failure. Use seeded runs to guarantee this.

### Acknowledge but deprioritize:
8. **#1 Extended thinking vs tool_choice** — Don't use extended thinking. Just use regular tool_use. Not a real tension for this project.
9. **#2 Time budget** — Tight but doable with CC. Focus on traces first.
10. **#5 Event boundaries** — Define: map_init, agent_turn, message_delivery, game_end as event types.
11. **#8 "Next turn" semantics** — Clarify: messages delivered at start of recipient's next activation.
12. **#10 Haiku reliability** — Start with haiku, fall back to sonnet if tool discipline is bad.
13. **#11 Langfuse as best-effort** — Langfuse is nice-to-have. JSON files are the real deliverable. If Langfuse setup takes >15min, skip it.

## Build Order (IMPLEMENTATION SEQUENCE)

### Phase 1: Simulation (~45min with CC)
1. `dungeon/world.py` — Grid, fog of war, items, obstacles, BFS solvability check, tool methods
2. `dungeon/agents.py` — Anthropic tool_use wrapper, simple system prompt
3. `dungeon/game.py` — Turn loop, message delivery, termination conditions

### Phase 2: Traces (~45min with CC)
4. `tracing/schema.py` — Event dataclass with all fields
5. `tracing/logger.py` — Langfuse integration + JSON file writer
6. Wire logger into game loop
7. `scripts/run_game.py` — Entry point with --runs N and --seed flags

### Phase 3: Generate Runs (~15min)
8. Run 5+ games, get mix of success/failure
9. Verify JSON output structure

### Phase 4: Viewer (~45min with CC)
10. `legibility/viewer.html` — Build around actual JSON structure from Phase 3
11. Timeline, grid, belief vs reality, divergence highlights

### Phase 5: Polish (~30min)
12. CLAUDE.md, README.md, TODOS.md
13. .gitignore (.env)
14. Multiple commits showing progression

## Files to Create
```
dungeon-agents/
├── CLAUDE.md
├── README.md
├── TODOS.md
├── requirements.txt
├── .env (NOT committed)
├── .gitignore
├── dungeon/
│   ├── __init__.py
│   ├── world.py
│   ├── agents.py
│   └── game.py
├── tracing/
│   ├── __init__.py
│   ├── schema.py
│   └── logger.py
├── legibility/
│   └── viewer.html
├── runs/
│   └── .gitkeep
└── scripts/
    └── run_game.py
```

## Error Handling Strategy
- World tool methods return `{"success": bool, "message": str, ...}` — NEVER throw for game state
- Anthropic API: retry 3x with backoff on rate limit, skip turn on timeout
- Unknown tool from LLM: return error result, log as tool_hallucination
- Langfuse down: warn and continue (JSON files are the real deliverable)
- Auth failure: fail fast at startup with clear message

## Grading Weights (from PDF)
- Judgment: 1st priority (smart scoping)
- Trace quality: 2nd (structured data, complete, well designed)
- Legibility: 3rd (viewer helps human understand)
- Taste: 4th (intentional, not AI-generated look)
- AI collaboration: 5th (conversation history shows driving toward good outcomes)

## Context
- Timebox started: April 9, 3:00 PM EST
- Assignment: Prove AI take-home
- Submission: GitHub repo, multiple run JSONs, 1-3min Loom, full AI chat logs

## Viewer Design Spec (from plan-design-review — 2026-04-09)

### Layout
- Two panels: left timeline (200px fixed), right detail (flex)
- Min viewport: 900px. Below that: "> viewport too narrow — resize to 900px+"
- No nav bars, no cards, no gradients

### CSS Variables
```css
:root {
  --bg: #0d1117;
  --panel-bg: #161b22;
  --border: #30363d;
  --text: #c9d1d9;
  --dim: #6e7681;
  --accent-a: #4488ff;
  --accent-b: #ff4444;
  --key: #f0c040;
  --door: #8b6914;
  --exit: #2d7a2d;
  --diverged: #ff6b6b;
  --font: 'JetBrains Mono', 'Fira Code', monospace;
}
```

### Timeline (left panel)
- Each turn: `[turn#] ● agent_id — tool_name` (normal)
- Diverged turns: `[turn#] ◆ agent_id — tool_name` in --diverged color
- On load: auto-scroll to first ◆ turn
- Keyboard: ArrowUp/Down navigate turns

### Detail pane (right panel) — hierarchy
1. Turn header: "Turn N — agent_id — tool_name" (top, monospace, large)
2. Dungeon grid: 8x8 div grid, cell types:
   - wall: #333, char "█"
   - empty: #1a1a2e, char "·"
   - key: --key, char "K"
   - door: --door, char "D"
   - exit: --exit, char "E"
   - agentA: --accent-a, char "A"
   - agentB: --accent-b, char "B"
3. Toggle button: "[ view: belief | ground truth ]" above grid. Space key toggles.
4. Tool I/O table: input → output, monochrome
5. Divergence banner (if diverged=true): red bg, shows divergence_reason
6. Belief state: key-value list, collapsible
7. LLM reasoning: scrollable <pre> block, collapsible

### Before-load state
- Centered: "> load trace file"
- File input styled as terminal command, label: "> load run"
- Blinking cursor

### Error state
- Red banner: "[ERROR] invalid trace format — expected run_id, turn fields"
- File input remains

### Border radius: 2px max everywhere
### No emoji — use Unicode text chars only (●, ◆, █, ·)

   295	            if position == self.exit_position
   296	        ]
   297	
   298	    def increment_turn(self) -> None:
   299	        self.turn_count += 1
   300	
   301	    def _generate_world(self) -> None:
   302	        for _ in range(self.max_generation_attempts):
   303	            candidate = self._build_candidate_layout()

   305	                self._apply_layout(candidate)

   307	        raise RuntimeError("failed to generate a solvable dungeon layout")

   309	    def _build_candidate_layout(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
   310	        all_positions = [(row, col) for row in range(self.size) for col in range(self.size)]
   311	        obstacle_count = self.rng.randint(*self.obstacle_range)
   312	        obstacles = set(self.rng.sample(all_positions, obstacle_count))
   313	
   314	        remaining_positions = [position for position in all_positions if position not in obstacles]
   315	        key_position, door_position, exit_position = self.rng.sample(remaining_positions, 3)
   316	
   317	        remaining_positions = [
   318	            position
   319	            for position in remaining_positions
   320	            if position not in {key_position, door_position, exit_position}
   321	        ]
   322	        agent_starts = self.rng.sample(remaining_positions, len(self.agent_ids))

   324	        return {

   326	            "key_position": key_position,
   327	            "door_position": door_position,

   329	            "agent_positions": dict(zip(self.agent_ids, agent_starts, strict=True)),
   330	        }
   331	
   332	    def _layout_is_solvable(self, candidate: dict[str, Any]) -> bool:
   333	        obstacles: set[Position] = candidate["obstacles"]
   334	        key_position: Position = candidate["key_position"]
   335	        door_position: Position = candidate["door_position"]
   336	        exit_position: Position = candidate["exit_position"]
   337	        agent_positions: dict[str, Position] = candidate["agent_positions"]
   338	
   339	        if any(
   340	            self._adjacent_positions(position) == []

+def build_system_prompt(agent_id: str, other_agent_id: str) -> str:
+    return (
+        f"You are {agent_id}, one of two explorers in a dungeon.\n"
+        f"Your teammate is {other_agent_id}.\n"
+        "Goal: both explorers must reach the exit. A locked door blocks progress and one "
+        "explorer must get the key and unlock the door.\n"
+        "You only know your own observable state. Fog of war is strict: you can only see "
+        "adjacent cells and any queued messages delivered at the start of your turn.\n"
+        "Pick exactly one tool each turn. Keep messages short and concrete. Prefer sharing "
+        "facts that remain useful even if your teammate receives them one turn later.\n"
+        "Do not invent map knowledge. If uncertain, observe or move conservatively."

+def call_llm(
+    agent_id: str,
+    other_agent_id: str,
+    observable_state: dict[str, Any],
+    conversation_history: list[dict[str, Any]],

+    system_message = {
+        "role": "system",
+        "content": build_system_prompt(agent_id=agent_id, other_agent_id=other_agent_id),
+    }

+

+            "llm_output_text": f"LLM error: {exc}",

+from __future__ import annotations
+
+from datetime import datetime, timezone
+import os
+import time
+from typing import Any
+
+from dungeon.agents import call_llm
+from dungeon.world import World
+from tracing.schema import AgentEvent
+
+
+def _determine_game_phase(world: World) -> str:
+    if world.door_unlocked and world.agents_at_exit():
+        return "exiting"
+    if world.door_unlocked:
+        return "door_unlocked"
+    if any("key" in inventory for inventory in world.agent_inventory.values()):
+        return "key_found"
+    return "exploring"
+
+
+def _divergence_reason(tool_name: str, tool_output: dict[str, Any]) -> str | None:
+    if tool_output.get("success") is True:
+        return None
+    if tool_name == "move":
+        return "move_blocked"
+    if tool_name == "pick_up":
+        return "item_missing"
+    if tool_name == "unlock_door":
+        return "door_locked"
+    if tool_name == "send_message":
+        return "message_failed"
+    return None
+
+
+def _execute_tool(
+    world: World,
+    agent_id: str,
+    other_agent_id: str,
+    tool_name: str,
+    tool_input: dict[str, Any],
+) -> dict[str, Any]:
+    try:
+        if tool_name == "move":
+            return world.move(agent_id, tool_input.get("direction", ""))
+        if tool_name == "pick_up":
+            return world.pick_up(agent_id, tool_input.get("item", ""))
+        if tool_name == "unlock_door":
+            return world.unlock_door(agent_id)
+        if tool_name == "send_message":
+            return world.send_message(
+                agent_id,
+                tool_input.get("to_agent", other_agent_id),
+                tool_input.get("message", ""),
+            )
+        if tool_name == "observe":
+            return world.observe(agent_id)
+    except Exception as exc:
+        return {"success": False, "message": str(exc)}
+
+    return {"success": False, "message": f"Unknown tool '{tool_name}'."}
+
+
+def run_game(
+    run_id: str,
+    seed: int | None = None,
+    turn_limit: int = 60,
+    logger: Any = None,
+) -> dict[str, Any]:
+    from openai import OpenAI
+
+    client = OpenAI(api_key=os.environ["OPENAI_API_KEY"])
+    world = World(random_seed=seed)
+    agent_ids = world.agent_ids
+    conversation_histories: dict[str, list[dict[str, Any]]] = {agent_id: [] for agent_id in agent_ids}
+    termination_reason: str | None = None
+    completed_turns = 0
+
+    for turn in range(1, turn_limit + 1):
+        completed_turns = turn
+        world.increment_turn()
+
+        active_agent = agent_ids[(turn - 1) % 2]
+        other_agent = agent_ids[1 - ((turn - 1) % 2)]
+
+        delivered = world.collect_messages(active_agent)
+        observable_state = world.get_observable_state(
+            active_agent,
+            delivered_messages=delivered,
+        )
+        llm_result = call_llm(
+            agent_id=active_agent,
+            other_agent_id=other_agent,
+            observable_state=observable_state,
+            conversation_history=conversation_histories[active_agent],
+            client=client,
+        )
+
+        tool_started_at = time.perf_counter()
+        tool_output = _execute_tool(
+            world=world,
+            agent_id=active_agent,
+            other_agent_id=other_agent,
+            tool_name=llm_result["tool_name"],
+            tool_input=llm_result["tool_input"],
+        )
+        tool_duration_ms = (time.perf_counter() - tool_started_at) * 1000
+
+        assistant_content = llm_result["llm_output_text"] or (
+            f"{llm_result['tool_name']} {llm_result['tool_input']}"
+        )
+        conversation_histories[active_agent].append(
+            {"role": "assistant", "content": assistant_content}
+        )
+
+        divergence_reason = _divergence_reason(llm_result["tool_name"], tool_output)
+        llm_input_messages = llm_result["llm_input_messages"]
+        last_user_message = next(
+            (
+                message.get("content", "")
+                for message in reversed(llm_input_messages)
+                if message.get("role") == "user"
+            ),
+            "",
+        )
+        tool_success = bool(tool_output.get("success"))
+
+        event = AgentEvent(
+            run_id=run_id,
+            turn=turn,
+            agent_id=active_agent,
+            timestamp=datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),
+            tool_name=llm_result["tool_name"],
+            tool_input=llm_result["tool_input"],
+            tool_output=tool_output,
+            tool_success=tool_success,
+            tool_duration_ms=tool_duration_ms,
+            llm_input_summary=str(last_user_message)[:200],
+            llm_output_text=llm_result["llm_output_text"],
+            llm_model=llm_result["llm_model"],
+            llm_latency_ms=llm_result["llm_latency_ms"],
+            llm_tokens_in=llm_result["llm_tokens_in"],
+            llm_tokens_out=llm_result["llm_tokens_out"],
+            belief_state=world.get_belief_state(active_agent),
+            world_state_snapshot=world.get_world_snapshot(),
+            diverged=not tool_output.get("success"),
+            divergence_reason=divergence_reason,
+            game_phase=_determine_game_phase(world),
+            agents_at_exit=world.agents_at_exit(),
+            turn_limit=turn_limit,
+            termination_reason=None,
+        )
+        if logger is not None:
+            logger.log_event(event)
+
+        status_text = "OK" if tool_success else "FAIL"
+        reason = divergence_reason or tool_output.get("message") or "-"
+        print(f"T{turn:03d} {active_agent} {llm_result['tool_name']} -> {status_text} [{reason}]")
+
+        if set(world.agents_at_exit()) == set(agent_ids):
+            termination_reason = "win"
+            break
+
+    if termination_reason is None:
+        termination_reason = "turn_limit"
+
+    if logger is not None:
+        logger.finalize(run_id, termination_reason)
+
+    return {
+        "run_id": run_id,
+        "seed": world.random_seed,
+        "turns": completed_turns,
+        "termination_reason": termination_reason,
+        "agents_at_exit": world.agents_at_exit(),
+    }

+from __future__ import annotations
+
+import json
+import os
+from pathlib import Path
+from typing import Any
+
+from tracing.schema import AgentEvent
+
+
+class TraceLogger:
+    def __init__(self, run_id: str, runs_dir: Path = Path("runs")) -> None:
+        self.run_id = run_id
+        self.runs_dir = runs_dir
+        self.runs_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
+        self.output_path = self.runs_dir / f"{run_id}.json"
+        self.events: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
+        self._langfuse = None
+        self._trace = None
+
+        try:
+            from langfuse import Langfuse
+
+            secret_key = os.environ.get("LANGFUSE_SECRET_KEY")
+            public_key = os.environ.get("LANGFUSE_PUBLIC_KEY")
+            host = os.environ.get("LANGFUSE_HOST", "https://cloud.langfuse.com")
+            if secret_key and public_key:
+                self._langfuse = Langfuse(
+                    secret_key=secret_key,
+                    public_key=public_key,
+                    host=host,
+                )
+                try:
+                    self._trace = self._langfuse.trace(
+                        id=run_id,
+                        name=f"dungeon-run-{run_id}",
+                    )
+                except Exception:
+                    self._trace = None
+        except Exception:
+            self._langfuse = None
+            self._trace = None
+
+    def log_event(self, event: AgentEvent) -> None:
+        event_dict = event.to_dict()
+        self.events.append(event_dict)
+
+        if self._trace is None:
+            return
+
+        try:
+            span = self._trace.span(
+                name=f"turn-{event.turn}",
+                input={
+                    "agent_id": event.agent_id,
+                    "tool_name": event.tool_name,
+                    "llm_input_summary": event.llm_input_summary,
+                },
+                output=event.tool_output,
+                metadata={
+                    "diverged": event.diverged,
+                    "divergence_reason": event.divergence_reason,
+                    "game_phase": event.game_phase,
+                },
+            )
+            span.generation(
+                name=f"llm-{event.turn}",
+                model=event.llm_model,
+                input=event.llm_input_summary,
+                output=event.llm_output_text,
+                metadata={
+                    "tool_name": event.tool_name,
+                    "tool_input": event.tool_input,
+                    "tool_success": event.tool_success,
+                    "tool_duration_ms": event.tool_duration_ms,
+                    "llm_latency_ms": event.llm_latency_ms,
+                    "llm_tokens_in": event.llm_tokens_in,
+                    "llm_tokens_out": event.llm_tokens_out,
+                },
+            )
+        except Exception:
+            return
+
+    def finalize(self, run_id: str, termination_reason: str) -> None:
+        if self.events:
+            self.events[-1]["termination_reason"] = termination_reason
+
+        payload = {
+            "run_id": run_id,
+            "termination_reason": termination_reason,
+            "total_turns": len(self.events),
+            "events": self.events,
+        }
+        with self.output_path.open("w", encoding="utf-8") as file_handle:
+            json.dump(payload, file_handle, indent=2, default=str)
+        print(f"[trace] wrote {self.output_path} ({len(self.events)} events)")
+
+        if self._langfuse is not None:
+            try:
+                self._langfuse.flush()
+            except Exception:
+                return

+#!/usr/bin/env python3

+from __future__ import annotations
+
+import argparse
+import uuid
+
+from dotenv import load_dotenv
+

+
+from dungeon.game import run_game
+from tracing.logger import TraceLogger
+
+
+def main() -> None:
+    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Run dungeon agent simulations.")
+    parser.add_argument("--runs", type=int, default=1)
+    parser.add_argument("--seed", type=int, default=None)
+    parser.add_argument("--turn-limit", type=int, default=60)
+    args = parser.parse_args()
+
+    results: list[dict[str, object]] = []
+    for index in range(args.runs):
+        run_id = f"run_{uuid.uuid4().hex[:8]}"
+        logger = TraceLogger(run_id)
+        run_seed = None if args.seed is None else args.seed + index
+        result = run_game(
+            run_id=run_id,
+            seed=run_seed,
+            turn_limit=args.turn_limit,
+            logger=logger,
+        )
+        results.append(result)
+
+    print("\nSummary")
+    print("run_id         seed        turns  result")
+    for result in results:
+        status = "WIN" if result["termination_reason"] == "win" else "TURN_LIMIT"
+        print(
+            f"{result['run_id']:<14} {result['seed']:<11} {result['turns']:<6} {status}"
+        )
+
+
+if __name__ == "__main__":
+    main()

+from __future__ import annotations

+from dungeon.world import DIRECTION_DELTAS, World

+def _free_adjacent_to_door(world: World) -> tuple[int, int]:
+    for position in world._adjacent_positions(world.door_position):  # noqa: SLF001
+        if position not in world.obstacles:
+            return position
+    raise AssertionError("expected at least one reachable cell adjacent to the door")
+
+

+        assert world.exit_position is not None

# Dungeon Agents

Two LLM agents (`gpt-4o-mini`) explore an 8x8 dungeon, produce structured traces, rendered in a legibility viewer.

## Running

```bash
pip install -r requirements.txt
cp .env.example .env  # add OPENAI_API_KEY
python scripts/run_game.py --runs 5 --turn-limit 60
```

## Structure

- `dungeon/world.py` — grid, fog-of-war, BFS solvability, tool methods
- `dungeon/agents.py` — OpenAI function calling wrapper
- `dungeon/game.py` — turn loop, message delivery, termination
- `tracing/schema.py` — AgentEvent dataclass (23 fields)
- `tracing/logger.py` — JSON file writer + Langfuse (best-effort)
- `legibility/viewer.html` — single-file trace viewer
- `runs/` — JSON trace files (gitignored except `.gitkeep`)
- `scripts/run_game.py` — CLI entry point

## Key decisions

- No LangGraph — custom loop for full trace control
- `diverged = not tool_output["success"]` — deterministic, never manufactured
- World enforces fog-of-war server-side (agents never get ground truth)
- JSON files are the real deliverable; Langfuse is best-effort

from __future__ import annotations

from dungeon.world import DIRECTION_DELTAS, World


def _free_adjacent_to_door(world: World) -> tuple[int, int]:
    for position in world._adjacent_positions(world.door_position):  # noqa: SLF001
        if position not in world.obstacles:
            return position
    raise AssertionError("expected at least one reachable cell adjacent to the door")


def test_world_generates_with_solvable_layout() -> None:
    for seed in range(20):
        world = World(random_seed=seed)
        assert world.key_position is not None
        assert world.door_position is not None
        assert world.exit_position is not None


def test_agent_starts_on_valid_non_obstacle_position() -> None:
    world = World(random_seed=1)
    for agent_id in world.agent_ids:
        assert world.agent_positions[agent_id] not in world.obstacles


def test_move_valid_direction_succeeds_for_at_least_one_direction_from_start() -> None:
    world = World(random_seed=7)
    agent_id = world.agent_ids[0]

    results = [world.move(agent_id, direction) for direction in DIRECTION_DELTAS]
    assert any(result["success"] for result in results)


def test_move_invalid_direction_returns_failure() -> None:
    world = World(random_seed=2)
    result = world.move(world.agent_ids[0], "northwest")
    assert result["success"] is False


def test_pick_up_at_wrong_position_returns_failure() -> None:
    world = World(random_seed=3)
    agent_id = world.agent_ids[0]
    world.agent_positions[agent_id] = (0, 0)
    world._update_agent_knowledge(agent_id)  # noqa: SLF001

    result = world.pick_up(agent_id, "key")
    assert result["success"] is False


def test_pick_up_at_key_position_returns_success_and_key_disappears() -> None:
    world = World(random_seed=4)
    agent_id = world.agent_ids[0]
    world.agent_positions[agent_id] = world.key_position
    world._update_agent_knowledge(agent_id)  # noqa: SLF001

    result = world.pick_up(agent_id, "key")
    assert result["success"] is True
    assert world.key_position is None
    assert "key" in world.agent_inventory[agent_id]


def test_unlock_door_without_key_returns_failure() -> None:
    world = World(random_seed=5)
    agent_id = world.agent_ids[0]
    world.agent_positions[agent_id] = _free_adjacent_to_door(world)
    world._update_agent_knowledge(agent_id)  # noqa: SLF001

    result = world.unlock_door(agent_id)
    assert result["success"] is False
    assert world.door_unlocked is False


def test_unlock_door_with_key_adjacent_to_door_returns_success() -> None:
    world = World(random_seed=6)
    agent_id = world.agent_ids[0]
    world.agent_inventory[agent_id].add("key")
    world.agent_positions[agent_id] = _free_adjacent_to_door(world)
    world._update_agent_knowledge(agent_id)  # noqa: SLF001

    result = world.unlock_door(agent_id)
    assert result["success"] is True
    assert world.door_unlocked is True


def test_send_message_and_collect_messages_round_trip() -> None:
    world = World(random_seed=7)
    sender, recipient = world.agent_ids

    result = world.send_message(sender, recipient, "found key soon")
    delivered = world.collect_messages(recipient)

    assert result["success"] is True
    assert delivered == [
        {"from_agent": sender, "to_agent": recipient, "content": "found key soon"}
    ]


def test_agents_at_exit_returns_correct_agents() -> None:
    world = World(random_seed=8)
    first_agent, second_agent = world.agent_ids
    world.agent_positions[first_agent] = world.exit_position
    world.agent_positions[second_agent] = (0, 0)
    world._update_agent_knowledge(first_agent)  # noqa: SLF001
    world._update_agent_knowledge(second_agent)  # noqa: SLF001

    assert world.agents_at_exit() == [first_agent]


def test_get_belief_state_has_required_keys() -> None:
    world = World(random_seed=9)
    belief_state = world.get_belief_state(world.agent_ids[0])

    assert set(belief_state) == {
        "position",
        "has_key",
        "door_unlocked",
        "known_cells",
        "messages_received",
        "goal",
    }


def test_get_world_snapshot_returns_eight_by_eight_grid_structure() -> None:
    world = World(random_seed=10)
    snapshot = world.get_world_snapshot()

    assert snapshot["size"] == 8
    assert len(snapshot["grid"]) == 8
    assert all(len(row) == 8 for row in snapshot["grid"])
    first_cell = snapshot["grid"][0][0]
    assert set(first_cell) == {"position", "terrain", "item", "occupants"}

#!/usr/bin/env python3

from __future__ import annotations

import argparse
import uuid

from dotenv import load_dotenv

load_dotenv()

from dungeon.game import run_game
from tracing.logger import TraceLogger


def main() -> None:
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Run dungeon agent simulations.")
    parser.add_argument("--runs", type=int, default=1)
    parser.add_argument("--seed", type=int, default=None)
    parser.add_argument("--turn-limit", type=int, default=60)
    args = parser.parse_args()

    results: list[dict[str, object]] = []
    for index in range(args.runs):
        run_id = f"run_{uuid.uuid4().hex[:8]}"
        logger = TraceLogger(run_id)
        run_seed = None if args.seed is None else args.seed + index
        result = run_game(
            run_id=run_id,
            seed=run_seed,
            turn_limit=args.turn_limit,
            logger=logger,
        )
        results.append(result)

    print("\nSummary")
    print("run_id         seed        turns  result")
    for result in results:
        status = "WIN" if result["termination_reason"] == "win" else "TURN_LIMIT"
        print(
            f"{result['run_id']:<14} {result['seed']:<11} {result['turns']:<6} {status}"
        )


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

from __future__ import annotations

from dataclasses import asdict, dataclass
from typing import Any


@dataclass
class AgentEvent:
    run_id: str
    turn: int
    agent_id: str
    timestamp: str
    tool_name: str
    tool_input: dict[str, Any]
    tool_output: dict[str, Any]
    tool_success: bool
    tool_duration_ms: float
    llm_input_summary: str
    llm_output_text: str
    llm_model: str
    llm_latency_ms: float
    llm_tokens_in: int
    llm_tokens_out: int
    belief_state: dict[str, Any]
    world_state_snapshot: dict[str, Any]
    diverged: bool
    divergence_reason: str | None
    game_phase: str
    agents_at_exit: list[str]
    turn_limit: int
    termination_reason: str | None

    def to_dict(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
        return asdict(self)

    96	def build_system_prompt(agent_id: str, other_agent_id: str) -> str:
    97	    return (

   101	        "explorer must get the key and unlock the door.\n"
   102	        "You only know your own observable state. Fog of war is strict: you can only see "
   103	        "adjacent cells and any queued messages delivered at the start of your turn.\n"
   104	        "Pick exactly one tool each turn. Keep messages short and concrete. Prefer sharing "
   105	        "facts that remain useful even if your teammate receives them one turn later.\n"
   106	        "Do not invent map knowledge. If uncertain, observe or move conservatively."
   107	    )

   110	def call_llm(
   111	    agent_id: str,

   113	    observable_state: dict[str, Any],
   114	    conversation_history: list[dict[str, Any]],
   115	    client: Any,
   116	) -> dict[str, Any]:

   118	        "role": "system",
   119	        "content": build_system_prompt(agent_id=agent_id, other_agent_id=other_agent_id),
   120	    }
   121	    user_message = {"role": "user", "content": json.dumps(observable_state)}
   122	    messages = [system_message, *conversation_history[-6:], user_message]

   134	        choice = response.choices[0]
   135	        message = choice.message
   136	        tool_calls = getattr(message, "tool_calls", None) or []
   137	        tool_call = tool_calls[0] if tool_calls else None
   138	
   139	        tool_name = "observe"
   140	        tool_input: dict[str, Any] = {}

     3	from datetime import datetime, timezone
     4	import os
     5	import time
